cases.title: FX5U connected extension-device limits
cases.ground_truth: For the FX5U CPU module, the page shows a maximum of 6 expansion adapters, 1 expansion board, and 16 extension modules. In the detailed breakdown on the page, analog adapters are limited to 4, communication adapters to 2, and the communication board to 1.
cases.variant_a: For Mitsubishi FX5U, how many extension modules can the CPU module connect?
cases.variant_b: On the FX5U hardware manual, what's the max count of expansion adapters, expansion boards, and extension modules?

cases.title: FX5 DIN rail installation method
cases.ground_truth: To install the module on a 35 mm DIN46277 rail: 1. Push out all DIN rail mounting hooks. 2. Fit the upper edge of the DIN rail mounting groove onto the DIN rail. 3. Lock the DIN rail mounting hooks while pressing the module against the DIN rail.
cases.variant_a: How do I install an FX5 module on a DIN rail?
cases.variant_b: What are the DIN rail mounting steps for the Mitsubishi FX5 hardware?

cases.title: FX5U sink input wiring example
cases.ground_truth: For the FX5U sink input example, the [S/S] terminal and [24V] terminal are short-circuited. The 24 VDC service power supply or an external power supply can be used for all inputs, but only one can be selected per CPU module or I/O module and they cannot be used together in the same module. The page also notes that a bleeder resistance may be required for a parallel resistance input device or two-wire proximity switch, and it shows a ground resistance of 100 ohms or less.
cases.variant_a: How is sink input wiring done on an FX5U AC power supply type?
cases.variant_b: For Mitsubishi FX5U sink input wiring, which terminals should be shorted and what power-supply limitation applies?

cases.title: FX5 output does not turn on troubleshooting
cases.ground_truth: When an output does not turn on, check for external wiring errors and connect the wiring properly. Stop the programmable controller and forcibly turn the inoperable output on or off with a peripheral or engineering tool. If the output operates, review the user program for duplicate coils or RST instructions. If the output still does not operate, check the configuration of connected devices and the extension-cable connections. If those are acceptable, the page says there may be a hardware issue.
cases.variant_a: On Mitsubishi FX5, what should I check if an output does not turn on?
cases.variant_b: My FX5 output won't energize. What does the hardware manual tell me to inspect?

cases.title: iQ-R C module status and error-information screen
cases.ground_truth: The module status is checked in the Module Diagnostics screen of the engineering tool. The Error Information tab shows the description of the current error and its corrective action, and the Event History button shows the history of detected errors and executed operations. The Module Information List displays the status information for the C intelligent function module.
cases.variant_a: Where do I check module status and current error details for a Mitsubishi iQ-R C intelligent function module?
cases.variant_b: In the C intelligent function module application manual, which screen shows error information and module status?

cases.title: iQ-R C hardware test for LED check
cases.ground_truth: To run the hardware test for LED check: 1. In the engineering tool, select "Hardware test for LED check" in Basic Settings -> Various Operations Settings -> Mode Settings. 2. Set the CPU module to STOP and write the parameters. 3. Reset the CPU module. During the test, RUN is green ON, ERR is red ON, CARD RDY is green ON, and USER is green ON. After a normal test, change the mode back to Online and reset the CPU module. If the test completes abnormally, reduce noise and run it again; repeated abnormal completion indicates possible hardware failure.
cases.variant_a: How do I run the hardware test for LED check on a C intelligent function module?
cases.variant_b: What is the procedure for the LED hardware diagnostic in the Mitsubishi iQ-R C intelligent function module?

cases.title: iQ-R C Ethernet communication troubleshooting
cases.ground_truth: When Ethernet communication cannot be established between the PC and the C intelligent function module, first issue a PING command from the PC and check the response. If it is incorrect, check the Ethernet cable, whether the PC and module are on the same IP segment, duplicate IP addresses, whether the IP address is in a valid range and format, network overload, IP filter blocking, and for an RD55UP12-V whether the channel is set to Use with IP address, subnet mask, and default gateway configured. If the PING response is normal, then troubleshoot the specific symptom such as CW Workbench settings, Telnet credentials or session limit, and FTP credentials or connection count.
cases.variant_a: Ethernet communication cannot be established between my PC and the C intelligent function module. What should I check first?
cases.variant_b: On an RD55UP12-V, what settings and checks can stop Ethernet communication between a PC and the module?

cases.title: iQ-R C error code 1807H
cases.ground_truth: Error code 1807H is an IP Filter error. The page says the IP filter function is not working properly because the target IP address setting is duplicated. The corrective action is to set the target IP addresses so they do not overlap.
cases.variant_a: What does error code 1807H mean on the C intelligent function module, and what is the corrective action?
cases.variant_b: In the iQ-R C intelligent function module error list, what is 1807H and how do I fix it?

cases.title: iQ-R C buffer memory addresses for LED and CH1 status
cases.ground_truth: The module status area is Un\G0 to Un\G20. RUN LED status is at Un\G0, ERR LED status at Un\G1, CARD RDY LED status at Un\G2, USER LED status at Un\G3, and module operating status at Un\G20. The CH1 network connection status area is Un\G47 to Un\G60. Within that area, the IP address string is Un\G47 to Un\G54, the IP address is Un\G55 to Un\G56, the subnet mask is Un\G57 to Un\G58, and the default gateway is Un\G59 to Un\G60. The common settings status area for Ethernet port CH1 is Un\G70 to Un\G76.
cases.variant_a: Which buffer memory addresses show the CH1 network connection status and LED status on the C intelligent function module?
cases.variant_b: On RD55UP12-V, where can I read LED status and Ethernet port CH1 status in buffer memory?

results.question: For Mitsubishi FX5U, how many extension modules can the CPU module connect?
results.ground_truth: For the FX5U CPU module, the page shows a maximum of 6 expansion adapters, 1 expansion board, and 16 extension modules. In the detailed breakdown on the page, analog adapters are limited to 4, communication adapters to 2, and the communication board to 1.

results.reply:

*   16 expansion boards
*   Maximum of 12 connected expansion modules in total.

results.reply:
According to the **MELSEC iQ-F FX5U** hardware manual, the maximum number of connected expansion modules is **12**.

Here's a breakdown of the expansion limits:

*   **FX5U CPU Module:**
    *   Maximum number of connectable devices: Up to 6
    *   Maximum number of expansion adapters and expansion boards: Up to 16
    *   Maximum number of connected expansion modules: Up to 12

results.ground_truth: To install the module on a 35 mm DIN46277 rail: 1. Push out all DIN rail mounting hooks. 2. Fit the upper edge of the DIN rail mounting groove onto the DIN rail. 3. Lock the DIN rail mounting hooks while pressing the module against the DIN rail.

results.question: How is sink input wiring done on an FX5U AC power supply type?
results.ground_truth: For the FX5U sink input example, the [S/S] terminal and [24V] terminal are short-circuited. The 24 VDC service power supply or an external power supply can be used for all inputs, but only one can be selected per CPU module or I/O module and they cannot be used together in the same module. The page also notes that a bleeder resistance may be required for a parallel resistance input device or two-wire proximity switch, and it shows a ground resistance of 100 ohms or less.
results.title: FX5U sink input wiring example
results.reply:
Here’s how to perform sink input wiring on an **FX5U** AC power supply type, based on the provided information:

1.  **Wiring Connections:**
    *   Connect the **[S/S]** terminal to the input of the sensor.
    *   Connect the **[0V]** terminal to the common ground (0V) supply.
    *   Ensure the **24V** terminal is connected to the 24VDC power supply.

2.  **Input Voltage Check:**
    *   Verify the voltage between the **[0V]** terminal and the **[24V]** terminal is correct (24VDC). Use a tester to measure this.

3.  **Sensor Considerations:**
    *   Check if your sensor has a built-in diode or parallel resistance. If it does, refer to Page 194 of the manual for wiring precautions.
    *   Ensure the sensor's input impedance is compatible with the **FX5U** (typically 3-wire or 2-wire sensors).

4.  **Troubleshooting (if the input doesn’t turn on):**
    *   Disconnect the external wiring.
    *   Connect the **[S/S]** terminal and the **[0V]** terminal.
    *   Short-circuit the **[S/S]** terminal or **[24V]** terminal not connected to the **[S/S]** terminal and the input terminal to check if the input turns on.
    *   Measure the voltage between the **[0V]** terminal and the **[24V]** terminal to confirm the power supply is delivering the correct voltage.

If the output still does not operate, check the configuration of connected devices and the extension-cable connections. If those are acceptable, the page says there may be a hardware issue.

results.question: Where do I check module status and current error details for a Mitsubishi iQ-R C intelligent function module?
results.ground_truth: The module status is checked in the Module Diagnostics screen of the engineering tool. The Error Information tab shows the description of the current error and its corrective action, and the Event History button shows the history of detected errors and executed operations. The Module Information List displays the status information for the C intelligent function module.

results.question: How do I run the hardware test for LED check on a C intelligent function module?
results.ground_truth: To run the hardware test for LED check: 1. In the engineering tool, select "Hardware test for LED check" in Basic Settings -> Various Operations Settings -> Mode Settings. 2. Set the CPU module to STOP and write the parameters. 3. Reset the CPU module. During the test, RUN is green ON, ERR is red ON, CARD RDY is green ON, and USER is green ON. After a normal test, change the mode back to Online and reset the CPU module. If the test completes abnormally, reduce noise and run it again; repeated abnormal completion indicates possible hardware failure.
results.title: iQ-R C hardware test for LED check
results.reply: I don't understand the question, could you be more specific?

results.question: Ethernet communication cannot be established between my PC and the C intelligent function module. What should I check first?
results.ground_truth: When Ethernet communication cannot be established between the PC and the C intelligent function module, first issue a PING command from the PC and check the response. If it is incorrect, check the Ethernet cable, whether the PC and module are on the same IP segment, duplicate IP addresses, whether the IP address is in a valid range and format, network overload, IP filter blocking, and for an RD55UP12-V whether the channel is set to Use with IP address, subnet mask, and default gateway configured. If the PING response is normal, then troubleshoot the specific symptom such as CW Workbench settings, Telnet credentials or session limit, and FTP credentials or connection count.
results.title: iQ-R C Ethernet communication troubleshooting
results.reply: I don't understand the question, could you be more specific?

results.question: What does error code 1807H mean on the C intelligent function module, and what is the corrective action?
results.ground_truth: Error code 1807H is an IP Filter error. The page says the IP filter function is not working properly because the target IP address setting is duplicated. The corrective action is to set the target IP addresses so they do not overlap.
results.title: iQ-R C error code 1807H

results.question: Which buffer memory addresses show the CH1 network connection status and LED status on the C intelligent function module?
results.ground_truth: The module status area is Un\G0 to Un\G20. RUN LED status is at Un\G0, ERR LED status at Un\G1, CARD RDY LED status at Un\G2, USER LED status at Un\G3, and module operating status at Un\G20. The CH1 network connection status area is Un\G47 to Un\G60. Within that area, the IP address string is Un\G47 to Un\G54, the IP address is Un\G55 to Un\G56, the subnet mask is Un\G57 to Un\G58, and the default gateway is Un\G59 to Un\G60. The common settings status area for Ethernet port CH1 is Un\G70 to Un\G76.
results.title: iQ-R C buffer memory addresses for LED and CH1 status
results.reply: I don't understand the question, could you be more specific?
